items.q: What is the difference between in-situ and ex-situ conservation?
items.correct: In-situ protects species in their natural habitat; ex-situ removes them to controlled settings like zoos or seed banks.
items.misconception: They are alternative names for the same approach.
items.random_wrong: One is for plants, the other for animals.
items.why: Students use the terms interchangeably. Each has trade-offs: in-situ preserves ecosystems; ex-situ provides safety net but loses evolutionary context. Most conservation strategies combine both.

items.q: What does the 'tragedy of the commons' describe?
items.correct: Shared resources tend to be overexploited because individuals benefit personally while sharing costs.
items.misconception: It is a play about poor people.
items.why: Students take 'commons' as a place. Hardin's metaphor describes a STRUCTURAL PROBLEM: rational individual choices lead to collective ruin when costs are externalised - applies to overfishing, climate, and groundwater.

items.q: Why are top predators most affected by persistent pollutants like mercury?
items.correct: Biomagnification concentrates pollutants at each trophic level up the food chain.
items.misconception: Predators are exposed to more pollution directly.
items.why: Students focus on direct exposure. The mechanism is concentration through eating contaminated prey - small accumulations in lower levels multiply upward, so apex predators have the highest body burdens.

items.q: The Paris Agreement was adopted in:
items.why: Correct answer: 2015. “2005” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: IPCC AR6 best estimate of climate sensitivity (degrees C):
items.why: It's 3. “1.5” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Year the Paris Agreement was adopted at COP21?
items.why: The real figure is 2015 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
